title: Topology Declarations, Policies, and Migration · Go Semantics Case: Topology Migration
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must make declaration equivalence, policy ownership, precondition errors, and rollback explicit in Go configuration; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the topology migration increment in a versioned Go topology ownership manifest. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Place operator-changeable TTL, limits, dead lettering, delivery limits, and queue behavior in policies instead of immutable client x-arguments where supported; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Topology Migration · Theory Topology Migration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 7372 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to protect declaration equivalence and route mutable changes through policy or migration; retain screen-reader-readable topology and delivery evidence; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Topology Migration contract for the supplied data.
